a.ru: SpringApplication.run() first detects the application type (SERVLET, REACTIVE, NONE) by checking for classes on the classpath. It creates the appropriate ApplicationContext type, locates and runs SpringApplicationRunListeners, prepares the Environment with properties and profiles, creates the context, loads bean definitions from the @SpringBootApplication class, triggers auto-configuration via @EnableAutoConfiguration, refreshes the context (BFPPs run, beans instantiate, BPPs process, embedded server starts), calls ApplicationRunner/CommandLineRunner beans, and finally publishes ApplicationReadyEvent. If anything fails, a FailureAnalyzer provides user-friendly error messages.
a.en: SpringApplication.run() first detects the application type (SERVLET, REACTIVE, NONE) by checking for classes on the classpath. It creates the appropriate ApplicationContext type, locates and runs SpringApplicationRunListeners, prepares the Environment with properties and profiles, creates the context, loads bean definitions from the @SpringBootApplication class, triggers auto-configuration via @EnableAutoConfiguration, refreshes the context (BFPPs run, beans instantiate, BPPs process, embedded server starts), calls ApplicationRunner/CommandLineRunner beans, and finally publishes ApplicationReadyEvent. If anything fails, a FailureAnalyzer provides user-friendly error messages.

summary.ru: Стартеры -- предварительно упакованные наборы зависимостей для обеспечения определенной функциональности. Автоконфигурация определяет, какие бины создать, основываясь на classpath и @Conditional аннотациях.
summary.en: Starters are pre-packaged dependency sets that provide specific functionality. Auto-configuration uses @Conditional annotations to determine which beans to create based on classpath contents and existing bean definitions.

deepDive.ru:
## Что такое стартер?

Стартер -- предварительно упакованные наборы зависимостей и сконфигурированных бинов для обеспечения определенной функциональности, например, доступа к базе данных или безопасности.

Classpath -- это путь, где среда выполнения JVM или инструменты сборки ищут нужные классы и библиотеки во время работы приложения. Когда запускается Java-приложение, JVM должна знать, где искать файлы .class или архивы .jar. Именно эти директории и jar-файлы называются classpath.

Автоконфигурация: Spring Boot автоматически определяет, какие бины и настройки нужно создать, ориентируясь на зависимости (стартеры) и наличие классов в classpath.
deepDive.en:
**Starters** are curated dependency descriptors. Adding a single starter dependency to your pom.xml/build.gradle pulls in all necessary libraries with compatible versions. Examples:

- `spring-boot-starter-web` -- embedded Tomcat, Spring MVC, Jackson JSON
- `spring-boot-starter-data-jpa` -- Hibernate, HikariCP, Spring Data JPA
- `spring-boot-starter-security` -- Spring Security with defaults
- `spring-boot-starter-test` -- JUnit 5, Mockito, AssertJ, MockMvc

Starters follow the naming convention `spring-boot-starter-{module}`. They don't contain code -- only dependency declarations in their POM.

**Auto-configuration** is the mechanism that reads the classpath and configures beans automatically. Each auto-configuration class uses conditional annotations:

- `@ConditionalOnClass` -- activate only if a class is on the classpath
- `@ConditionalOnMissingBean` -- activate only if the user hasn't defined this bean
- `@ConditionalOnProperty` -- activate based on application.properties values
- `@ConditionalOnBean` -- activate if a specific bean exists
- `@ConditionalOnJava` -- activate for specific Java versions
- `@ConditionalOnResource` -- activate if a specific resource exists

The key principle is **opinionated defaults with easy overrides**: auto-configuration provides sensible defaults, but defining your own bean always takes precedence (via @ConditionalOnMissingBean). You can exclude auto-configurations with `@SpringBootApplication(exclude = ...)` or `spring.autoconfigure.exclude` in properties.

To create a custom starter, provide a `-autoconfigure` artifact with @Configuration classes and register them in `META-INF/spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports`.

tip.ru: Run your app with `--debug` flag or set `debug=true` in properties to see the auto-configuration report -- it shows which configurations were applied and which were skipped, and why.
tip.en: Запустите приложение с флагом `--debug` или `debug=true` в properties, чтобы увидеть отчет автоконфигурации -- какие конфигурации применены, какие пропущены и почему.

q.ru: What is a Spring Boot starter? Give examples of commonly used starters.
q.en: What is a Spring Boot starter? Give examples of commonly used starters.

a.ru: A starter is a pre-packaged set of compatible dependencies for specific functionality. Examples: spring-boot-starter-web (embedded Tomcat + Spring MVC + Jackson), spring-boot-starter-data-jpa (Hibernate + HikariCP + Spring Data JPA), spring-boot-starter-security (Spring Security defaults), spring-boot-starter-test (JUnit 5 + Mockito + AssertJ). Starters contain no code, only dependency declarations.
a.en: A starter is a pre-packaged set of compatible dependencies for specific functionality. Examples: spring-boot-starter-web (embedded Tomcat + Spring MVC + Jackson), spring-boot-starter-data-jpa (Hibernate + HikariCP + Spring Data JPA), spring-boot-starter-security (Spring Security defaults), spring-boot-starter-test (JUnit 5 + Mockito + AssertJ). Starters contain no code, only dependency declarations.

q.ru: How does Spring Boot auto-configuration decide which beans to create? How can you override it?
q.en: How does Spring Boot auto-configuration decide which beans to create? How can you override it?

a.ru: Auto-configuration classes use @Conditional annotations: @ConditionalOnClass checks if classes are on the classpath, @ConditionalOnMissingBean checks if the user already defined a bean, @ConditionalOnProperty checks application properties. To override: define your own @Bean (takes precedence via @ConditionalOnMissingBean), exclude auto-configurations via @SpringBootApplication(exclude=...), or set properties to disable features (e.g., spring.autoconfigure.exclude).
a.en: Auto-configuration classes use @Conditional annotations: @ConditionalOnClass checks if classes are on the classpath, @ConditionalOnMissingBean checks if the user already defined a bean, @ConditionalOnProperty checks application properties. To override: define your own @Bean (takes precedence via @ConditionalOnMissingBean), exclude auto-configurations via @SpringBootApplication(exclude=...), or set properties to disable features (e.g., spring.autoconfigure.exclude).

q.ru: How would you create a custom Spring Boot starter for a company-internal library?
q.en: How would you create a custom Spring Boot starter for a company-internal library?

a.ru: Create two modules: (1) an autoconfigure module with @AutoConfiguration classes using @Conditional annotations, registered in META-INF/spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports; (2) a starter module that depends on the autoconfigure module plus the library itself. The auto-configuration should use @ConditionalOnMissingBean so users can override defaults, @ConditionalOnClass for optional features, and @ConfigurationProperties for externalized configuration. Include spring-boot-configuration-processor for IDE support of properties.
a.en: Create two modules: (1) an autoconfigure module with @AutoConfiguration classes using @Conditional annotations, registered in META-INF/spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports; (2) a starter module that depends on the autoconfigure module plus the library itself. The auto-configuration should use @ConditionalOnMissingBean so users can override defaults, @ConditionalOnClass for optional features, and @ConfigurationProperties for externalized configuration. Include spring-boot-configuration-processor for IDE support of properties.

summary.ru: Профили позволяют активировать различные конфигурации бинов в зависимости от окружения (dev, test, prod). Активный профиль задается через spring.profiles.active, а бины помечаются аннотацией @Profile.
summary.en: Profiles allow activating different bean configurations per environment (dev, test, prod). The active profile is set via spring.profiles.active, and beans are annotated with @Profile to be conditionally loaded.

deepDive.ru:
## Профилирование

Одним из решений множественного внедрения является профилирование. У запускаемого приложения всегда есть атрибут профилирования -- это список активных профилей, под которым запускается приложение.

По умолчанию активный профиль только один -- default. Но мы можем кастомизировать, указав при помощи properties файла: spring.profiles.active="профиль"

Аннотация @Profile, принимающая строковое значение, позволяет конфигурировать компонент только при определенном профиле приложения.

Также есть возможность создавать бины в зависимости от какого-либо условия. Для этого используется аннотация @Conditional и ее производные:
- @ConditionalOnBean -- true при наличии заданного Bean
- @ConditionalOnClass -- true при наличии заданного класса
- @ConditionalOnJava -- true при верной версии Java
- @ConditionalOnProperty -- true при выполнении условия в файле свойств
- @ConditionalOnResource -- true при наличии заданного resource
deepDive.en:
Spring Profiles provide a way to segregate parts of your application configuration and make it available only in certain environments.

**Activating profiles** can be done via:
- `spring.profiles.active=dev,metrics` in application.properties
- JVM argument: `-Dspring.profiles.active=prod`
- Environment variable: `SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=prod`
- Programmatically: `SpringApplication.setAdditionalProfiles("dev")`

**Profile-specific properties files** follow the pattern `application-{profile}.properties` (or .yml). When a profile is active, its properties file is loaded and overrides values from the base `application.properties`. For example, application-dev.yml might set a local H2 database while application-prod.yml points to PostgreSQL.

**@Profile annotation** can be placed on @Component, @Configuration, or @Bean methods:
- `@Profile("dev")` -- active only when "dev" profile is active
- `@Profile("!prod")` -- active when "prod" is NOT active
- `@Profile({"dev", "test"})` -- active when either profile is active

**@Conditional** annotations provide even finer control beyond profiles. `@Conditional` takes a Condition interface with a `boolean matches(ConditionContext, AnnotatedTypeMetadata)` method. ConditionContext provides access to BeanDefinitionRegistry, BeanFactory, Environment, ResourceLoader, and ClassLoader.

Best practice: Use profiles for environment-specific configuration (database URLs, API keys, feature flags) and @Conditional for capability-based configuration (class availability, bean presence).

tip.ru: Use `application-{profile}.properties` files for environment-specific values and `@Profile` on beans that need completely different implementations per environment.
tip.en: Используйте файлы `application-{profile}.properties` для значений, специфичных для окружения, и `@Profile` на бинах, которым нужны полностью разные реализации для каждого окружения.

q.ru: What are Spring profiles and how do you activate them?
q.en: What are Spring profiles and how do you activate them?

a.ru: Profiles allow environment-specific bean configuration. Activate via: spring.profiles.active in application.properties, JVM argument -Dspring.profiles.active=prod, environment variable SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE, or programmatically. Profile-specific properties files (application-{profile}.properties) override base properties. @Profile annotation restricts beans to specific profiles.
a.en: Profiles allow environment-specific bean configuration. Activate via: spring.profiles.active in application.properties, JVM argument -Dspring.profiles.active=prod, environment variable SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE, or programmatically. Profile-specific properties files (application-{profile}.properties) override base properties. @Profile annotation restricts beans to specific profiles.

q.ru: How do profile-specific properties files work? What is the loading order?
q.en: How do profile-specific properties files work? What is the loading order?

a.ru: Spring loads application.properties first (base), then application-{activeProfile}.properties which overrides matching keys. For multiple active profiles (e.g., dev,metrics), later profiles take precedence. application-default.properties is loaded only when no explicit profile is active. External property sources (environment variables, command-line args) override all file-based properties. Spring Boot also supports application.yml with multi-document blocks separated by ---.
a.en: Spring loads application.properties first (base), then application-{activeProfile}.properties which overrides matching keys. For multiple active profiles (e.g., dev,metrics), later profiles take precedence. application-default.properties is loaded only when no explicit profile is active. External property sources (environment variables, command-line args) override all file-based properties. Spring Boot also supports application.yml with multi-document blocks separated by ---.

q.ru: Explain the @Conditional mechanism. How would you create a custom condition?
q.en: Explain the @Conditional mechanism. How would you create a custom condition?

a.ru: Implement the Condition interface with matches(ConditionContext, AnnotatedTypeMetadata). ConditionContext gives access to the BeanDefinitionRegistry, BeanFactory, Environment, ResourceLoader, and ClassLoader. AnnotatedTypeMetadata provides annotation information on the target. Create a custom annotation meta-annotated with @Conditional(YourCondition.class). Spring evaluates conditions during bean definition registration, before instantiation. Conditions can check classpath, properties, OS, existing beans, or any runtime state. Spring Boot's @ConditionalOn* annotations are all built on this mechanism.
a.en: Implement the Condition interface with matches(ConditionContext, AnnotatedTypeMetadata). ConditionContext gives access to the BeanDefinitionRegistry, BeanFactory, Environment, ResourceLoader, and ClassLoader. AnnotatedTypeMetadata provides annotation information on the target. Create a custom annotation meta-annotated with @Conditional(YourCondition.class). Spring evaluates conditions during bean definition registration, before instantiation. Conditions can check classpath, properties, OS, existing beans, or any runtime state. Spring Boot's @ConditionalOn* annotations are all built on this mechanism.

summary.ru: Множественное внедрение возникает, когда для одного типа есть несколько бинов. Решается через @Primary (основной бин по умолчанию), @Qualifier (выбор конкретного бина по имени) и @Order (приоритет в коллекции).
summary.en: Multiple bean injection occurs when several beans match one type. Resolved via @Primary (default bean), @Qualifier (select by name), and @Order (priority in collections).

deepDive.ru:
## Множественное внедрение

Это ситуация, когда необходимо иметь более одного бина определенного типа. Возникает ошибка DI, которая говорит что невозможно точно определить бин для внедрения зависимостей. Возникает из-за того, что система ищет кандидата по типу, а тип является интерфейсом и она находит несколько реализаций данного интерфейса.

Способы решения:
- **@Primary** -- используется для указания основного бина контейнера. Если есть несколько кандидатов и ни один не указан через @Qualifier, будет выбран бин с @Primary.
- **@Order** -- для указания порядка (приоритета) бинов в коллекции, используемой для внедрения зависимостей. Принимает целое число -- индекс в списке.
- **@Qualifier** -- позволяет выбрать конкретный компонент. Принимает строковое значение идентификатора компонента.
deepDive.en:
When Spring finds multiple beans of the same type during dependency injection, it throws `NoUniqueBeanDefinitionException`. This commonly happens with interface-based design where multiple implementations exist.

**Resolution strategies:**

1. **@Primary** -- marks one bean as the default choice. When no other disambiguation is provided, the @Primary bean wins. Best for a clear "main" implementation with optional alternatives.

2. **@Qualifier("name")** -- explicitly selects a specific bean by its qualifier name. More precise than @Primary and takes precedence over it. Can be placed on constructor parameters, setter parameters, or fields.

3. **@Order(value)** -- controls the order of beans when injecting a `List<T>`. Lower values have higher priority. Does NOT affect which single bean is chosen -- only the ordering in collections.

4. **Parameter name matching** -- as a fallback, Spring matches the parameter/field name to a bean name. If you have a field `private PaymentService stripePaymentService`, Spring will look for a bean named "stripePaymentService".

5. **Custom @Qualifier annotations** -- create a custom annotation annotated with @Qualifier for type-safe bean selection. This avoids magic strings and is refactoring-friendly.

6. **Profiles** -- use @Profile to activate different implementations per environment, avoiding multiple candidates at runtime.

Precedence order: @Qualifier > @Primary > parameter name matching. If none resolves the ambiguity, Spring throws an exception.

tip.ru: Use `@Primary` for the main implementation and `@Qualifier` only where you need a specific alternative. For collections, `@Order` controls the sequence.
tip.en: Используйте `@Primary` для основной реализации и `@Qualifier` только там, где нужна конкретная альтернатива. Для коллекций `@Order` управляет порядком.

q.ru: What happens when Spring finds multiple beans of the same type? How do you resolve it?
q.en: What happens when Spring finds multiple beans of the same type? How do you resolve it?

a.ru: Spring throws NoUniqueBeanDefinitionException. Resolve with: @Primary on the default bean, @Qualifier("name") to select a specific bean, or inject a List<T> to get all implementations. @Qualifier takes precedence over @Primary. As a fallback, Spring matches parameter/field names to bean names.
a.en: Spring throws NoUniqueBeanDefinitionException. Resolve with: @Primary on the default bean, @Qualifier("name") to select a specific bean, or inject a List<T> to get all implementations. @Qualifier takes precedence over @Primary. As a fallback, Spring matches parameter/field names to bean names.

q.ru: What is the precedence order when resolving bean ambiguity? When would you use @Primary vs @Qualifier?
q.en: What is the precedence order when resolving bean ambiguity? When would you use @Primary vs @Qualifier?

a.ru: @Qualifier has highest precedence, then @Primary, then parameter name matching. Use @Primary when there's a clear 'main' implementation that most injection points should use (e.g., the primary DataSource). Use @Qualifier when specific injection points need a non-default implementation (e.g., a secondary read-only DataSource). They complement each other: @Primary provides the default, @Qualifier provides the override.
a.en: @Qualifier has highest precedence, then @Primary, then parameter name matching. Use @Primary when there's a clear 'main' implementation that most injection points should use (e.g., the primary DataSource). Use @Qualifier when specific injection points need a non-default implementation (e.g., a secondary read-only DataSource). They complement each other: @Primary provides the default, @Qualifier provides the override.

q.ru: How would you design a plugin system where multiple implementations of an interface are discovered and ordered at runtime?
q.en: How would you design a plugin system where multiple implementations of an interface are discovered and ordered at runtime?

a.ru: Define the plugin interface and have implementations annotated with @Component and @Order. Inject List<PluginInterface> -- Spring auto-discovers all implementations and orders them by @Order value. For more control, use a custom @Qualifier annotation with metadata (e.g., @Plugin(name="pdf", priority=1)). Combine with @Conditional to enable/disable plugins via properties. For true runtime discovery, use Spring's SpringFactoriesLoader or ServiceLoader pattern. The List injection approach is how Spring Security's filter chain and Spring Boot's auto-configuration ordering work internally.
a.en: Define the plugin interface and have implementations annotated with @Component and @Order. Inject List<PluginInterface> -- Spring auto-discovers all implementations and orders them by @Order value. For more control, use a custom @Qualifier annotation with metadata (e.g., @Plugin(name="pdf", priority=1)). Combine with @Conditional to enable/disable plugins via properties. For true runtime discovery, use Spring's SpringFactoriesLoader or ServiceLoader pattern. The List injection approach is how Spring Security's filter chain and Spring Boot's auto-configuration ordering work internally.

summary.ru: Жизненный цикл запроса в Spring MVC: запрос поступает к DispatcherServlet, HandlerMapping находит контроллер, HandlerAdapter вызывает метод, контроллер выполняет бизнес-логику, ответ формируется и отправляется клиенту.
summary.en: Spring MVC request lifecycle: request hits DispatcherServlet, HandlerMapping locates the controller, HandlerAdapter invokes the method, the controller executes business logic, the response is formed and sent to the client.

deepDive.ru:
## Жизненный цикл запроса в Spring MVC

Spring MVC -- это модуль Spring Framework для построения веб-приложений на основе модели MVC (Model-View-Controller):
- **Model** -- хранит данные и бизнес-логику
- **View** -- отвечает за отображение данных
- **Controller** -- обрабатывает пользовательские запросы

Этапы обработки запроса:

1. **Поступление запроса к DispatcherServlet** -- главный контроллер (front controller), который обрабатывает все входящие HTTP-запросы
2. **Поиск контроллера (HandlerMapping)** -- DispatcherServlet обращается к HandlerMapping для определения контроллера. HandlerMapping возвращает объект, включающий контроллер и метод для вызова
3. **Вызов обработчика через HandlerAdapter** -- адаптирует запрос к API контроллера
4. **Выполнение бизнес-логики в контроллере**
5. **Формирование ответа** -- ответ контроллера преобразуется в JSON или другой формат
6. **Обработка исключений** -- @ExceptionHandler или @ControllerAdvice перехватывают ошибки
7. **Отправка ответа клиенту**
8. **Интерцепторы** -- на различных этапах могут предварительно или послеобрабатывать запросы
deepDive.en:
The DispatcherServlet is the front controller of Spring MVC, receiving every HTTP request and orchestrating the entire processing pipeline:

**Step 1 -- DispatcherServlet receives the request**: As a servlet, it's mapped to `/` or `/*` and intercepts all incoming requests.

**Step 2 -- HandlerMapping resolution**: DispatcherServlet consults HandlerMapping implementations to find which controller method handles the URL. `RequestMappingHandlerMapping` processes @RequestMapping annotations. It returns a HandlerExecutionChain containing the handler and any interceptors.

**Step 3 -- Interceptor preHandle**: Any registered HandlerInterceptor.preHandle() methods run. If any returns false, processing stops.

**Step 4 -- HandlerAdapter invocation**: The adapter (typically RequestMappingHandlerAdapter) resolves method arguments (@RequestParam, @PathVariable, @RequestBody via HttpMessageConverters), invokes the controller method, and processes the return value.

**Step 5 -- Controller execution**: Your business logic runs. The method returns a response object, ResponseEntity, or view name.

**Step 6 -- Response processing**: For @RestController, the return value is converted to JSON/XML via HttpMessageConverter (Jackson by default). For @Controller, ViewResolver finds and renders the view.

**Step 7 -- Exception handling**: If an exception occurs, @ExceptionHandler methods (local or @ControllerAdvice global) handle it and produce an error response.

**Step 8 -- Interceptor postHandle/afterCompletion**: Post-processing runs after the handler, and afterCompletion runs regardless of exceptions.

**Step 9 -- Response sent to client**: The servlet container sends the HTTP response back.

tip.ru: Use `@RestControllerAdvice` for global exception handling across all controllers -- it keeps error handling logic centralized and consistent.
tip.en: Используйте `@RestControllerAdvice` для глобальной обработки исключений во всех контроллерах -- это централизует и унифицирует обработку ошибок.

q.ru: Describe the request lifecycle in Spring MVC. What is the role of DispatcherServlet?
q.en: Describe the request lifecycle in Spring MVC. What is the role of DispatcherServlet?

a.ru: DispatcherServlet is the front controller that receives all HTTP requests. It consults HandlerMapping to find the target controller, uses HandlerAdapter to invoke the method, processes the response (JSON conversion or view rendering), handles exceptions via @ExceptionHandler/@ControllerAdvice, and runs interceptors at pre/post stages. It coordinates the entire MVC pipeline.
a.en: DispatcherServlet is the front controller that receives all HTTP requests. It consults HandlerMapping to find the target controller, uses HandlerAdapter to invoke the method, processes the response (JSON conversion or view rendering), handles exceptions via @ExceptionHandler/@ControllerAdvice, and runs interceptors at pre/post stages. It coordinates the entire MVC pipeline.

q.ru: What is the difference between HandlerInterceptor and a Servlet Filter? When would you use each?
q.en: What is the difference between HandlerInterceptor and a Servlet Filter? When would you use each?

a.ru: Servlet Filters operate at the servlet container level, before DispatcherServlet, and work with raw request/response objects. HandlerInterceptors operate within Spring MVC, after DispatcherServlet, with access to the handler method and Spring context. Use Filters for cross-cutting concerns like encoding, CORS, security (Spring Security uses filters). Use Interceptors for MVC-specific logic like logging handler execution time, permission checks that need handler metadata, or modifying ModelAndView.
a.en: Servlet Filters operate at the servlet container level, before DispatcherServlet, and work with raw request/response objects. HandlerInterceptors operate within Spring MVC, after DispatcherServlet, with access to the handler method and Spring context. Use Filters for cross-cutting concerns like encoding, CORS, security (Spring Security uses filters). Use Interceptors for MVC-specific logic like logging handler execution time, permission checks that need handler metadata, or modifying ModelAndView.

q.ru: How does Spring MVC resolve controller method arguments? Explain the role of HandlerMethodArgumentResolver.
q.en: How does Spring MVC resolve controller method arguments? Explain the role of HandlerMethodArgumentResolver.

a.ru: RequestMappingHandlerAdapter maintains a list of HandlerMethodArgumentResolvers. For each method parameter, it iterates the resolvers calling supportsParameter(). The matching resolver extracts the value: RequestParamMethodArgumentResolver handles @RequestParam, PathVariableMethodArgumentResolver handles @PathVariable, RequestResponseBodyMethodProcessor handles @RequestBody (delegating to HttpMessageConverter for deserialization). Custom resolvers can be registered for domain-specific parameter types. The resolution order matters -- first matching resolver wins.
a.en: RequestMappingHandlerAdapter maintains a list of HandlerMethodArgumentResolvers. For each method parameter, it iterates the resolvers calling supportsParameter(). The matching resolver extracts the value: RequestParamMethodArgumentResolver handles @RequestParam, PathVariableMethodArgumentResolver handles @PathVariable, RequestResponseBodyMethodProcessor handles @RequestBody (delegating to HttpMessageConverter for deserialization). Custom resolvers can be registered for domain-specific parameter types. The resolution order matters -- first matching resolver wins.

summary.ru: @Controller -- обработчик веб-запросов с возвратом представления. @RestController объединяет @Controller и @ResponseBody для возврата данных в JSON/XML. @RequestMapping и производные (@GetMapping, @PostMapping и т.д.) определяют маршруты URL.
summary.en: @Controller handles web requests returning views. @RestController combines @Controller + @ResponseBody for JSON/XML data responses. @RequestMapping and its shortcuts (@GetMapping, @PostMapping, etc.) define URL routes.

deepDive.ru:
## Контроллеры в Spring MVC

Классы контроллеров помечаются аннотациями:
- **@Controller** -- регистрирует класс как контроллер, обработчик веб-запросов. Возвращаемое значение интерпретируется как имя представления (view name).
- **@RestController** -- объединяет @Controller и @ResponseBody. Используется для RESTful контроллеров, методы которых по умолчанию возвращают данные в виде JSON или XML.
- **@RequestMapping** -- определяет общий маршрут URL.

Методы-обработчики помечаются аннотациями:
- @RequestMapping и производные: @GetMapping, @PostMapping, @PutMapping, @DeleteMapping, @PatchMapping
- @RequestParam -- для связывания параметров запроса с параметрами метода
- @PathVariable -- встраивает значения из URL в параметры метода
- @RequestBody -- связывает тело запроса с параметром метода (десериализация JSON)
- @ResponseBody -- возвращаемое значение метода записывается в тело ответа
deepDive.en:
**@Controller vs @RestController**: @Controller is for traditional MVC where methods return view names resolved by ViewResolver (Thymeleaf, JSP). @RestController is a convenience annotation that adds @ResponseBody to every method, so return values are serialized directly to the response body via HttpMessageConverter (Jackson for JSON).

**Request mapping annotations**:
- `@RequestMapping(value = "/api", method = RequestMethod.GET)` -- base annotation
- `@GetMapping("/users")` -- shorthand for GET requests
- `@PostMapping("/users")` -- shorthand for POST requests
- `@PutMapping("/users/{id}")` -- full resource update
- `@PatchMapping("/users/{id}")` -- partial resource update
- `@DeleteMapping("/users/{id}")` -- resource deletion

**Parameter binding annotations**:
- `@PathVariable` -- extracts from URL path: `/users/{id}`
- `@RequestParam` -- extracts from query string: `?name=John&age=30`
- `@RequestBody` -- deserializes the HTTP body (typically JSON) into a Java object
- `@RequestHeader` -- extracts HTTP header values
- `@CookieValue` -- extracts cookie values
- `@ModelAttribute` -- binds form data to an object

**ResponseEntity** gives full control over the HTTP response: status code, headers, and body. It's preferred over @ResponseStatus for dynamic responses.

tip.ru: Use `ResponseEntity` for full control over HTTP status codes, headers, and body in REST APIs instead of just returning objects directly.
tip.en: Используйте `ResponseEntity` для полного контроля над HTTP статус-кодами, заголовками и телом ответа в REST API вместо простого возврата объектов.

q.ru: What is the difference between @Controller and @RestController?
q.en: What is the difference between @Controller and @RestController?

a.ru: @Controller marks a class as a web controller where method return values are interpreted as view names. @RestController is @Controller + @ResponseBody combined -- method return values are automatically serialized to JSON/XML and written to the response body. Use @Controller for server-rendered pages (Thymeleaf), @RestController for REST APIs.
a.en: @Controller marks a class as a web controller where method return values are interpreted as view names. @RestController is @Controller + @ResponseBody combined -- method return values are automatically serialized to JSON/XML and written to the response body. Use @Controller for server-rendered pages (Thymeleaf), @RestController for REST APIs.

q.ru: Explain the difference between @RequestParam, @PathVariable, and @RequestBody. When do you use each?
q.en: Explain the difference between @RequestParam, @PathVariable, and @RequestBody. When do you use each?

a.ru: @PathVariable extracts values from the URL path (/users/{id}) -- used for resource identifiers. @RequestParam extracts query string parameters (?page=0&size=20) -- used for filtering, sorting, pagination. @RequestBody deserializes the entire HTTP body (JSON/XML) into a Java object -- used for POST/PUT payloads. @PathVariable and @RequestParam handle simple values; @RequestBody handles complex objects. All three can be combined in one method.
a.en: @PathVariable extracts values from the URL path (/users/{id}) -- used for resource identifiers. @RequestParam extracts query string parameters (?page=0&size=20) -- used for filtering, sorting, pagination. @RequestBody deserializes the entire HTTP body (JSON/XML) into a Java object -- used for POST/PUT payloads. @PathVariable and @RequestParam handle simple values; @RequestBody handles complex objects. All three can be combined in one method.

q.ru: How does Spring MVC content negotiation work? How does it decide between JSON and XML responses?
q.en: How does Spring MVC content negotiation work? How does it decide between JSON and XML responses?

a.ru: Spring uses ContentNegotiationManager which checks (in order): URL path extension (.json/.xml, disabled by default in newer versions), query parameter (?format=json), Accept header (application/json vs application/xml). The matching HttpMessageConverter handles serialization -- MappingJackson2HttpMessageConverter for JSON, Jaxb2RootElementHttpMessageConverter for XML. You can configure preferred media types, default content type, and strategy precedence via WebMvcConfigurer.configureContentNegotiation(). @RequestMapping(produces = "application/json") narrows a handler to specific media types.
a.en: Spring uses ContentNegotiationManager which checks (in order): URL path extension (.json/.xml, disabled by default in newer versions), query parameter (?format=json), Accept header (application/json vs application/xml). The matching HttpMessageConverter handles serialization -- MappingJackson2HttpMessageConverter for JSON, Jaxb2RootElementHttpMessageConverter for XML. You can configure preferred media types, default content type, and strategy precedence via WebMvcConfigurer.configureContentNegotiation(). @RequestMapping(produces = "application/json") narrows a handler to specific media types.

summary.ru: Валидация данных в Spring MVC выполняется через Bean Validation API: поля аннотируются @NotNull, @Size, @Min и т.д., а в контроллере указывается @Valid. @Validated расширяет @Valid поддержкой групп валидации. Ошибки собираются в BindingResult.
summary.en: Spring MVC validation uses Bean Validation API: fields annotated with @NotNull, @Size, @Min, etc., and @Valid triggers validation in the controller. @Validated extends @Valid with validation group support. Errors are collected in BindingResult.

deepDive.ru:
## Валидация данных в Spring MVC

**Использование Bean Validation API**: Аннотируем поля сущностей аннотациями: @NotNull, @Min, @Max, @Size, @Pattern и т.д. В параметре метода контроллера необходимо указать @Valid, чтобы применились ограничения. Также есть @Validated, который позволяет использовать расширенные валидации (группы).

**Кастомный валидатор**: Реализация интерфейса Validator для сложных правил, которые не покрываются стандартными аннотациями.
deepDive.en:
**Bean Validation (JSR 380)** is the standard Java validation API, implemented by Hibernate Validator (included via spring-boot-starter-web). Key annotations:

- `@NotNull` -- field must not be null
- `@NotBlank` -- string must not be null, empty, or whitespace
- `@NotEmpty` -- collection/string must not be null or empty
- `@Size(min, max)` -- string length or collection size bounds
- `@Min(value)` / `@Max(value)` -- numeric bounds
- `@Pattern(regexp)` -- string must match regex
- `@Email` -- valid email format
- `@Past` / `@Future` -- date constraints
- `@Positive` / `@Negative` -- sign constraints

**Triggering validation**: Place `@Valid` or `@Validated` before the `@RequestBody` parameter. If validation fails, Spring throws `MethodArgumentNotValidException` (for @RequestBody) or `BindException` (for form data).

**@Valid vs @Validated**: `@Valid` (javax.validation) triggers validation recursively on nested objects. `@Validated` (Spring) supports validation groups -- you can define different validation rules for create vs update operations.

**BindingResult**: Must be declared immediately after the validated parameter. When present, Spring does not throw an exception but populates BindingResult with errors, letting you handle them manually.

**Custom validators**: Implement `ConstraintValidator<A, T>` for custom annotations. Define the annotation with @Constraint(validatedBy = YourValidator.class) and implement isValid() with your logic.

**Cross-field validation**: Use class-level constraints to validate relationships between fields (e.g., password matches confirmPassword).

tip.ru: Always use `@NotBlank` for strings instead of `@NotNull` -- it also rejects empty and whitespace-only values, which is almost always what you want.
tip.en: Всегда используйте `@NotBlank` для строк вместо `@NotNull` -- она также отклоняет пустые строки и строки из пробелов, что почти всегда является нужным поведением.

q.ru: How does validation work in Spring MVC? What annotations trigger it?
q.en: How does validation work in Spring MVC? What annotations trigger it?

a.ru: Annotate DTO fields with Bean Validation annotations (@NotNull, @Size, @Email, etc.) and place @Valid before the @RequestBody parameter in the controller. If validation fails, Spring throws MethodArgumentNotValidException. Use @RestControllerAdvice to handle this globally and return structured error responses.
a.en: Annotate DTO fields with Bean Validation annotations (@NotNull, @Size, @Email, etc.) and place @Valid before the @RequestBody parameter in the controller. If validation fails, Spring throws MethodArgumentNotValidException. Use @RestControllerAdvice to handle this globally and return structured error responses.

q.ru: What is the difference between @Valid and @Validated? When would you use validation groups?
q.en: What is the difference between @Valid and @Validated? When would you use validation groups?

a.ru: @Valid (javax.validation) triggers standard validation and cascades to nested objects. @Validated (Spring) supports validation groups -- different sets of constraints for different operations. Example: during creation, 'id' must be null; during update, 'id' must be provided. Define marker interfaces (OnCreate, OnUpdate), assign them to constraint annotations via groups={}, and use @Validated(OnCreate.class) in the controller.
a.en: @Valid (javax.validation) triggers standard validation and cascades to nested objects. @Validated (Spring) supports validation groups -- different sets of constraints for different operations. Example: during creation, 'id' must be null; during update, 'id' must be provided. Define marker interfaces (OnCreate, OnUpdate), assign them to constraint annotations via groups={}, and use @Validated(OnCreate.class) in the controller.

q.ru: How would you implement cross-field validation (e.g., password confirmation) using Bean Validation?
q.en: How would you implement cross-field validation (e.g., password confirmation) using Bean Validation?

a.ru: Create a class-level constraint annotation (e.g., @PasswordMatches) with @Constraint(validatedBy = PasswordMatchesValidator.class). The validator implements ConstraintValidator<PasswordMatches, RegistrationRequest> and checks both fields in isValid(). Place the annotation on the DTO class. For programmatic cross-field validation, use BindingResult with a custom Validator implementation. Spring also supports method-level validation with @Validated on the service class and @Valid on method parameters for service-layer validation.
a.en: Create a class-level constraint annotation (e.g., @PasswordMatches) with @Constraint(validatedBy = PasswordMatchesValidator.class). The validator implements ConstraintValidator<PasswordMatches, RegistrationRequest> and checks both fields in isValid(). Place the annotation on the DTO class. For programmatic cross-field validation, use BindingResult with a custom Validator implementation. Spring also supports method-level validation with @Validated on the service class and @Valid on method parameters for service-layer validation.

summary.ru: Загрузка файлов в Spring MVC реализуется через MultipartFile. Spring автоматически разбирает multipart/form-data запросы. Размер файлов настраивается через spring.servlet.multipart.*. Properties файлы (.properties / .yml) хранят конфигурацию в формате ключ-значение; значения внедряются через @Value или Environment.
summary.en: File upload in Spring MVC uses MultipartFile. Spring automatically parses multipart/form-data requests. File size limits are configured via spring.servlet.multipart.*. Properties files (.properties / .yml) store key-value configuration; values are injected via @Value or the Environment interface.

deepDive.ru:
## Загрузка файлов (MultiPart Request)

Это механизм, позволяющий клиентам отправлять файлы. Spring автоматически разбирает такие запросы и предоставляет объект MultipartFile, с которым можно работать.

**Настройка размера файлов:**
- `spring.servlet.multipart.max-file-size: 10MB` -- ограничение на одиночный файл
- `spring.servlet.multipart.max-request-size: 10MB` -- ограничение на все файлы в запросе

Для отправки frontend должен устанавливать Content-Type: `multipart/form-data`.

## Свойства проекта. Properties файлы

**Properties file** -- текстовый файл для хранения конфигурационных параметров в формате ключ-значение.

Виды: `*.properties` и `*.yml` (отличаются синтаксисом).

- **@PropertySource** -- добавляет источник пар свойств ключ-значение в среду Spring (используется вместе с @Configuration)
- **@Value** -- внедрение значений из properties файла
- **Environment** -- интерфейс, предоставляющий доступ к профилям и свойствам приложения. Является компонентом контекста, поэтому можно внедрять в другие компоненты.
deepDive.en:
## File Upload (MultiPart Request)

Spring MVC handles file uploads via `MultipartFile` interface. The embedded server (Tomcat) parses the multipart request automatically. Key configuration properties:
- `spring.servlet.multipart.enabled=true` (default)
- `spring.servlet.multipart.max-file-size=10MB`
- `spring.servlet.multipart.max-request-size=10MB`
- `spring.servlet.multipart.file-size-threshold=2KB` (threshold after which files are written to disk)

The client must send the request with `Content-Type: multipart/form-data`. Multiple files can be uploaded using `MultipartFile[]` or `List<MultipartFile>`.

## Properties Files

Spring Boot supports two configuration formats: `.properties` (flat key=value) and `.yml` (hierarchical YAML). Both are loaded from `application.properties` / `application.yml` in the classpath.

**Property injection mechanisms:**
- `@Value("${property.key}")` -- inject a single property into a field
- `@ConfigurationProperties(prefix)` -- bind a group of properties to a POJO (type-safe configuration)
- `Environment.getProperty("key")` -- programmatic access
- `@PropertySource("classpath:custom.properties")` -- load additional property files

**Profile-specific properties:** `application-{profile}.yml` is loaded when the profile is active. Set via `spring.profiles.active=dev`.

**Property precedence (highest to lowest):** command-line args > environment variables > application-{profile}.yml > application.yml > defaults.

tip.ru: Используйте `@ConfigurationProperties` вместо множества `@Value` -- это безопаснее по типам и удобнее для группировки связанных настроек.
tip.en: Use `@ConfigurationProperties` instead of many `@Value` annotations -- it is type-safe and better for grouping related settings.

q.ru: How do you handle file uploads in Spring Boot? What is MultipartFile?
q.en: How do you handle file uploads in Spring Boot? What is MultipartFile?

a.ru: Add @RequestParam("file") MultipartFile to a controller method. MultipartFile provides getOriginalFilename(), getSize(), getContentType(), getInputStream(), and transferTo() for saving. The client sends multipart/form-data. Configure max sizes via spring.servlet.multipart.max-file-size and max-request-size in application.yml. Spring auto-configures a MultipartResolver (StandardServletMultipartResolver) with Spring Boot.
a.en: Add @RequestParam("file") MultipartFile to a controller method. MultipartFile provides getOriginalFilename(), getSize(), getContentType(), getInputStream(), and transferTo() for saving. The client sends multipart/form-data. Configure max sizes via spring.servlet.multipart.max-file-size and max-request-size in application.yml. Spring auto-configures a MultipartResolver (StandardServletMultipartResolver) with Spring Boot.

q.ru: What is the difference between @Value, @ConfigurationProperties, and Environment for reading properties?
q.en: What is the difference between @Value, @ConfigurationProperties, and Environment for reading properties?

a.ru: @Value("${key}") injects a single property -- simple but scattered across classes. @ConfigurationProperties(prefix) binds a group of properties to a POJO -- type-safe, supports validation with @Validated, and centralizes related config. Environment is a programmatic API for dynamic property access at runtime. Best practice: use @ConfigurationProperties for structured config groups, @Value for one-off values, and Environment when properties are determined at runtime.
a.en: @Value("${key}") injects a single property -- simple but scattered across classes. @ConfigurationProperties(prefix) binds a group of properties to a POJO -- type-safe, supports validation with @Validated, and centralizes related config. Environment is a programmatic API for dynamic property access at runtime. Best practice: use @ConfigurationProperties for structured config groups, @Value for one-off values, and Environment when properties are determined at runtime.

q.ru: Explain Spring Boot's property resolution order and how profile-specific configuration works.
q.en: Explain Spring Boot's property resolution order and how profile-specific configuration works.

a.ru: Spring Boot loads properties in a defined precedence order (higher overrides lower): (1) command-line arguments, (2) JNDI attributes, (3) Java system properties, (4) OS environment variables, (5) application-{profile}.properties/yml, (6) application.properties/yml, (7) @PropertySource annotations, (8) default properties. Profile-specific files (application-dev.yml) activate when spring.profiles.active=dev is set. You can also use @Profile on beans for conditional registration. Multiple profiles can be active simultaneously, and properties from all active profiles are merged.
a.en: Spring Boot loads properties in a defined precedence order (higher overrides lower): (1) command-line arguments, (2) JNDI attributes, (3) Java system properties, (4) OS environment variables, (5) application-{profile}.properties/yml, (6) application.properties/yml, (7) @PropertySource annotations, (8) default properties. Profile-specific files (application-dev.yml) activate when spring.profiles.active=dev is set. You can also use @Profile on beans for conditional registration. Multiple profiles can be active simultaneously, and properties from all active profiles are merged.

summary.ru: Entity -- класс-представление таблицы в БД. Аннотируется @Entity, @Table, @Id, @GeneratedValue, @Column. Связи между сущностями: @OneToOne, @OneToMany, @ManyToOne, @ManyToMany. Атрибуты связей: mappedBy, fetch (LAZY/EAGER), cascade, orphanRemoval.
summary.en: An Entity is a class that maps to a database table. Annotated with @Entity, @Table, @Id, @GeneratedValue, @Column. Relationships: @OneToOne, @OneToMany, @ManyToOne, @ManyToMany. Key attributes: mappedBy, fetch (LAZY/EAGER), cascade, orphanRemoval.

deepDive.ru:
## Entity

Entity -- это класс, который является представлением таблицы или представления из БД. Правила создания:
- **@Entity** -- класс является сущностью
- **@Table** -- указывает для какой таблицы в БД эта сущность
- **@Id** -- поле является идентификатором
- **@GeneratedValue** -- стратегия автоматического формирования идентификатора (AUTO, IDENTITY, SEQUENCE, TABLE)
- **@Column** -- настройки столбца (name, nullable, unique, length)

## Отношения между Entity

- **@OneToOne** -- fetch по умолчанию: EAGER
- **@OneToMany / @ManyToOne** -- @OneToMany fetch: LAZY; @ManyToOne: EAGER
- **@ManyToMany** -- fetch по умолчанию: LAZY

**Атрибуты связей:**
- `mappedBy` -- указывает поле владельца связи на не-владеющей стороне
- `fetch` -- стратегия загрузки: LAZY или EAGER
- `cascade` -- операции каскадно (ALL, PERSIST, MERGE, REMOVE, REFRESH, DETACH)
- `orphanRemoval` -- удалять сиротские сущности

**@JoinColumn** -- указывает столбец внешнего ключа
**@JoinTable** -- создаёт промежуточную таблицу связей (для @ManyToMany)
deepDive.en:
## JPA Entity

A JPA Entity is a POJO that maps to a relational database table. Requirements: must have `@Entity`, a no-arg constructor, must not be final, and must have an `@Id` field.

**ID Generation strategies** (`@GeneratedValue(strategy = ...)`):
- `IDENTITY` -- database auto-increment. Prevents batch inserts.
- `SEQUENCE` -- database sequence. Supports batch inserts via allocationSize.
- `TABLE` -- simulates sequence using a table. Portable but slower.
- `AUTO` -- provider chooses the best strategy.

## Entity Relationships

**Owning side vs Inverse side:** The owning side holds the FK. The inverse side uses `mappedBy`. Only changes from the owning side are persisted.

**Cascade types:** PERSIST, MERGE, REMOVE, REFRESH, DETACH, ALL.

**Fetch strategies:** EAGER loads immediately. LAZY loads on first access (proxy). Best practice: LAZY everywhere, eagerly fetch only when needed.

**orphanRemoval=true:** Removing a child from the parent's collection deletes it from the DB.

tip.ru: Всегда используйте LAZY загрузку по умолчанию и подтягивайте данные через JOIN FETCH или @EntityGraph только когда нужно.
tip.en: Always default to LAZY fetching and eagerly load data via JOIN FETCH or @EntityGraph only when actually needed.

q.ru: What is a JPA Entity and what annotations are required to map it to a database table?
q.en: What is a JPA Entity and what annotations are required to map it to a database table?

a.ru: A JPA Entity is a Java class that represents a database table. Required annotations: @Entity on the class, @Id on the primary key field. Common: @Table(name) for table name, @GeneratedValue for auto-generated IDs, @Column for column settings. The class must have a no-arg constructor and must not be final.
a.en: A JPA Entity is a Java class that represents a database table. Required annotations: @Entity on the class, @Id on the primary key field. Common: @Table(name) for table name, @GeneratedValue for auto-generated IDs, @Column for column settings. The class must have a no-arg constructor and must not be final.

q.ru: Explain the difference between owning side and inverse side in JPA relationships. Why does it matter?
q.en: Explain the difference between owning side and inverse side in JPA relationships. Why does it matter?

a.ru: The owning side has the foreign key column and controls persistence. The inverse side uses mappedBy. Only changes on the owning side are saved -- adding a child only to the inverse side's collection without setting the owning side means the relationship is not persisted. For @OneToMany, the @ManyToOne side is the owner. Always sync both sides using helper methods.
a.en: The owning side has the foreign key column and controls persistence. The inverse side uses mappedBy. Only changes on the owning side are saved -- adding a child only to the inverse side's collection without setting the owning side means the relationship is not persisted. For @OneToMany, the @ManyToOne side is the owner. Always sync both sides using helper methods.

q.ru: What are the trade-offs between CascadeType.REMOVE and orphanRemoval? When would you use each?
q.en: What are the trade-offs between CascadeType.REMOVE and orphanRemoval? When would you use each?

a.ru: CascadeType.REMOVE cascades delete when the parent is deleted. orphanRemoval=true also deletes a child when removed from the parent's collection, even if the parent is not deleted. Example: removing an item from order.getItems() with orphanRemoval=true DELETEs it from DB. With only CASCADE.REMOVE, removing from collection just breaks the relationship. Use orphanRemoval for composition relationships. Be cautious with CascadeType.ALL on @ManyToMany.
a.en: CascadeType.REMOVE cascades delete when the parent is deleted. orphanRemoval=true also deletes a child when removed from the parent's collection, even if the parent is not deleted. Example: removing an item from order.getItems() with orphanRemoval=true DELETEs it from DB. With only CASCADE.REMOVE, removing from collection just breaks the relationship. Use orphanRemoval for composition relationships. Be cautious with CascadeType.ALL on @ManyToMany.

summary.ru: Spring Data JPA предоставляет интерфейсы-репозитории: Repository, CrudRepository, PagingAndSortingRepository, JpaRepository. Запросы создаются через имя метода, @Query (JPQL/Native), CriteriaAPI или QueryDSL. @Modifying помечает UPDATE/DELETE запросы.
summary.en: Spring Data JPA provides repository interfaces: Repository, CrudRepository, PagingAndSortingRepository, JpaRepository. Queries via method names, @Query (JPQL/Native), CriteriaAPI, or QueryDSL. @Modifying marks UPDATE/DELETE queries.

deepDive.ru:
## Repository

Интерфейсы для взаимодействия с базой данных.

**Основные типы:**
- **Repository** -- базовый маркерный интерфейс
- **CrudRepository** -- CRUD операции (save, findById, findAll, delete)
- **PagingAndSortingRepository** -- постраничный вывод и сортировка
- **JpaRepository** -- расширяет все + flush, batch операции

**@Modifying** -- для UPDATE/DELETE запросов, чтобы Spring Data понял, что возвращается количество строк.

**Блокировки:**
- **Оптимистическая** (@Version) -- проверка при обновлении. OptimisticLockException.
- **Пессимистическая** (@Lock) -- блокировка на уровне БД.

**Способы запросов:** Method Name, @Query (JPQL/Native), CriteriaAPI, QueryDSL.
deepDive.en:
## Spring Data Repository Hierarchy

`JpaRepository<T, ID>` extends `PagingAndSortingRepository` extends `CrudRepository` extends `Repository`.

## Query Methods

**Method Name derivation:** `findByNameAndEmail(String, String)` becomes `WHERE name=? AND email=?`.

**@Query (JPQL):** `@Query("SELECT u FROM User u WHERE u.email = :email")` -- entity model.

**@Query (Native):** `nativeQuery = true` -- raw SQL, bypasses Persistence Context.

**@Modifying:** Required for UPDATE/DELETE. Add `clearAutomatically = true` to sync PC.

**CriteriaAPI:** Type-safe programmatic queries via CriteriaBuilder, CriteriaQuery, Root, Predicate.

**QueryDSL:** Simpler syntax via generated Q-classes and BooleanBuilder.

tip.ru: Используйте `@Modifying(clearAutomatically = true)` для UPDATE/DELETE запросов, чтобы Persistence Context синхронизировался.
tip.en: Use `@Modifying(clearAutomatically = true)` for UPDATE/DELETE queries to auto-sync the Persistence Context.

q.ru: What is the difference between CrudRepository, JpaRepository, and PagingAndSortingRepository?
q.en: What is the difference between CrudRepository, JpaRepository, and PagingAndSortingRepository?

a.ru: CrudRepository provides basic CRUD (save, findById, delete, findAll). PagingAndSortingRepository adds pagination and sorting. JpaRepository extends both with JPA-specific methods like flush(), saveAndFlush(), deleteInBatch(). In practice, extend JpaRepository to get all features.
a.en: CrudRepository provides basic CRUD (save, findById, delete, findAll). PagingAndSortingRepository adds pagination and sorting. JpaRepository extends both with JPA-specific methods like flush(), saveAndFlush(), deleteInBatch(). In practice, extend JpaRepository to get all features.

q.ru: What are the different ways to write queries in Spring Data JPA?
q.en: What are the different ways to write queries in Spring Data JPA?

a.ru: 1) Method name derivation (findByNameAndEmail) -- simple, auto-generated. 2) @Query with JPQL -- entity model, portable. 3) @Query nativeQuery=true -- raw SQL for DB-specific features. 4) CriteriaAPI -- programmatic, type-safe, dynamic. 5) QueryDSL -- like CriteriaAPI but simpler via Q-classes. Use method names for simple lookups, JPQL for custom queries, native for performance-critical, CriteriaAPI/QueryDSL for dynamic filters.
a.en: 1) Method name derivation (findByNameAndEmail) -- simple, auto-generated. 2) @Query with JPQL -- entity model, portable. 3) @Query nativeQuery=true -- raw SQL for DB-specific features. 4) CriteriaAPI -- programmatic, type-safe, dynamic. 5) QueryDSL -- like CriteriaAPI but simpler via Q-classes. Use method names for simple lookups, JPQL for custom queries, native for performance-critical, CriteriaAPI/QueryDSL for dynamic filters.

q.ru: Explain optimistic vs pessimistic locking in JPA. When would you choose each?
q.en: Explain optimistic vs pessimistic locking in JPA. When would you choose each?

a.ru: Optimistic: @Version field, checks version on update. OptimisticLockException on conflict. Best for low-contention. Pessimistic: @Lock acquires DB lock at read time -- PESSIMISTIC_READ allows reads, PESSIMISTIC_WRITE blocks both. Choose pessimistic for frequent conflicts or critical data (financial). Optimistic has better throughput; pessimistic provides stronger guarantees.
a.en: Optimistic: @Version field, checks version on update. OptimisticLockException on conflict. Best for low-contention. Pessimistic: @Lock acquires DB lock at read time -- PESSIMISTIC_READ allows reads, PESSIMISTIC_WRITE blocks both. Choose pessimistic for frequent conflicts or critical data (financial). Optimistic has better throughput; pessimistic provides stronger guarantees.

summary.ru: Persistence Context -- кэш первого уровня, управляемый EntityManager. Сущности проходят через состояния: Transient (не связана с PC), Persistent/Managed (связана, имеет ID), Detached (отсоединена от PC), Removed (запланирована к удалению).
summary.en: Persistence Context is a first-level cache managed by EntityManager. Entities pass through states: Transient (not associated with PC), Persistent/Managed (associated, has ID), Detached (disconnected from PC), Removed (scheduled for deletion).

deepDive.ru:
## Persistence Context

- **DataSource** -- интерфейс настроек подключения к БД
- **EntityManager** -- основной интерфейс для управления сущностями (Entity)

Persistence Context -- кэш первого уровня Hibernate. Все сущности в рамках транзакции хранятся в нём. Повторный запрос возвращает объект из кэша.

## Entity Lifecycle

- **Transient** -- экземпляр создан, не связан с PC
- **Persistent (Managed)** -- связан с PC, имеет ID, соответствует строке в БД
- **Detached** -- имеет ID, но не связан с PC
- **Removed** -- имеет ID, связан с PC, запланирован к удалению

## Кэширование Hibernate

- **First Level Cache** -- Persistence Context (привязан к сессии/транзакции)
- **Second Level Cache** -- общий для приложения (EhCache, Infinispan)
deepDive.en:
## Persistence Context (First-Level Cache)

Key behaviors:
- **Identity guarantee:** One Java object per DB row within a PC.
- **Dirty checking:** Hibernate compares entity state with snapshot at flush time.
- **Write-behind:** SQL batched and executed at flush (before commit, before queries, or on explicit flush()).

## Entity Lifecycle States

- **Transient:** `new User()` -- not in PC. persist() moves to Managed.
- **Managed:** Tracked by Hibernate. Changes auto-detected and flushed.
- **Detached:** PC was closed. merge() re-attaches.
- **Removed:** remove() called -- DELETE scheduled at flush.

**EntityManager methods:** persist(), merge(), remove(), find(), detach(), flush(), clear().

## Second-Level Cache

Shared across sessions. Per-entity with @Cacheable. Useful for read-heavy, rarely-changing data.

tip.ru: Для read-only операций используйте `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` -- Hibernate пропускает dirty checking.
tip.en: For read-only operations use `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` -- Hibernate skips dirty checking.

q.ru: What is the Persistence Context in JPA?
q.en: What is the Persistence Context in JPA?

a.ru: The Persistence Context is a first-level cache storing managed entities within a transaction. EntityManager is the API for it. Every entity loaded is cached in the PC. Repeated lookups by ID return the same object (identity guarantee). Changes to managed entities are auto-detected (dirty checking) and synced with DB at flush/commit.
a.en: The Persistence Context is a first-level cache storing managed entities within a transaction. EntityManager is the API for it. Every entity loaded is cached in the PC. Repeated lookups by ID return the same object (identity guarantee). Changes to managed entities are auto-detected (dirty checking) and synced with DB at flush/commit.

q.ru: Describe the four JPA Entity lifecycle states and transitions between them.
q.en: Describe the four JPA Entity lifecycle states and transitions between them.

a.ru: 1) Transient: created with 'new', not in PC. persist() -> Managed. 2) Managed: in PC, tracked. Changes auto-synced via dirty checking. 3) Detached: was managed but PC closed or detach() called. merge() re-attaches. 4) Removed: remove() called, DELETE scheduled. Key: only Managed entities are tracked. After detach, changes are lost unless merge() is called.
a.en: 1) Transient: created with 'new', not in PC. persist() -> Managed. 2) Managed: in PC, tracked. Changes auto-synced via dirty checking. 3) Detached: was managed but PC closed or detach() called. merge() re-attaches. 4) Removed: remove() called, DELETE scheduled. Key: only Managed entities are tracked. After detach, changes are lost unless merge() is called.

q.ru: How does Hibernate dirty checking work? How can you optimize it?
q.en: How does Hibernate dirty checking work? How can you optimize it?

a.ru: When loaded, Hibernate stores a field snapshot. At flush, it compares every managed entity's current state with its snapshot. O(N*M) comparisons can be expensive. Optimizations: (1) @Transactional(readOnly=true) skips dirty checking. (2) Use DTO projections for reads. (3) Detach unneeded entities. (4) StatelessSession for bulk ops. (5) @DynamicUpdate generates UPDATE only for changed columns.
a.en: When loaded, Hibernate stores a field snapshot. At flush, it compares every managed entity's current state with its snapshot. O(N*M) comparisons can be expensive. Optimizations: (1) @Transactional(readOnly=true) skips dirty checking. (2) Use DTO projections for reads. (3) Detach unneeded entities. (4) StatelessSession for bulk ops. (5) @DynamicUpdate generates UPDATE only for changed columns.

summary.ru: Транзакции в Spring управляются программно или декларативно (@Transactional). Основные атрибуты: propagation (REQUIRED, REQUIRES_NEW, NESTED и др.) и isolation (READ_UNCOMMITTED, READ_COMMITTED, REPEATABLE_READ, SERIALIZABLE). По умолчанию откат на RuntimeException.
summary.en: Spring transactions are managed programmatically or declaratively (@Transactional). Key attributes: propagation (REQUIRED, REQUIRES_NEW, NESTED, etc.) and isolation levels. Rolls back on RuntimeException by default.

deepDive.ru:
## Transaction

В Spring Framework транзакции обрабатываются через Spring Transaction Management.

**Два механизма:**
- **Программное** -- connection.setAutoCommit(false), commit()
- **Декларативное** -- @Transactional

## Propagation

- **REQUIRED** (по умолчанию) -- если TX есть, выполняется в ней; если нет -- создаётся
- **SUPPORTS** -- если TX есть -- в ней; если нет -- без транзакции
- **REQUIRES_NEW** -- всегда новая TX; текущая приостанавливается
- **NOT_SUPPORTED** -- без TX; текущая приостанавливается
- **MANDATORY** -- обязательна TX; иначе исключение
- **NEVER** -- TX запрещена; иначе исключение
- **NESTED** -- вложенная TX (savepoint)

## Isolation

- DEFAULT, READ_UNCOMMITTED, READ_COMMITTED, REPEATABLE_READ, SERIALIZABLE
deepDive.en:
## How @Transactional Works

Spring creates a proxy. On external call, the proxy starts TX, invokes method, commits or rolls back.

**Self-invocation gotcha:** Calling @Transactional from same class bypasses proxy -- no TX created. Fix: inject self, use TransactionTemplate, or separate beans.

## Propagation Details

- **REQUIRED:** Shared TX. Rollback in inner rolls back everything.
- **REQUIRES_NEW:** Independent TX. Inner commits even if outer fails. Use for audit logs.
- **NESTED:** JDBC savepoints. Nested rollback only undoes nested changes.

## Rollback Behavior

Default: rollback on unchecked exceptions (RuntimeException) and Errors. NOT on checked exceptions. Override with rollbackFor.

## Read-Only

`@Transactional(readOnly = true)` -- skips dirty checking, optimizes JDBC/DB for reads.

tip.ru: @Transactional не работает при вызове метода внутри того же класса (self-invocation) -- самая частая ошибка.
tip.en: @Transactional does not work for self-invocation (calling a method within the same class) -- the most common pitfall.

q.ru: What does @Transactional do in Spring? What happens on RuntimeException?
q.en: What does @Transactional do in Spring? What happens on RuntimeException?

a.ru: Spring creates a proxy that starts a transaction before the method and commits after success. On RuntimeException (unchecked) or Error, the transaction rolls back automatically. Checked exceptions do NOT trigger rollback by default -- use rollbackFor = Exception.class to change this.
a.en: Spring creates a proxy that starts a transaction before the method and commits after success. On RuntimeException (unchecked) or Error, the transaction rolls back automatically. Checked exceptions do NOT trigger rollback by default -- use rollbackFor = Exception.class to change this.

q.ru: Explain the difference between REQUIRED, REQUIRES_NEW, and NESTED propagation.
q.en: Explain the difference between REQUIRED, REQUIRES_NEW, and NESTED propagation.

a.ru: REQUIRED (default): joins existing TX or creates new. Inner failure rolls back everything. REQUIRES_NEW: always new, independent TX. Inner commits even if outer fails -- use for audit logs. NESTED: savepoint within existing TX. Nested rollback only undoes nested changes; outer can catch and continue. Requires JDBC savepoint support.
a.en: REQUIRED (default): joins existing TX or creates new. Inner failure rolls back everything. REQUIRES_NEW: always new, independent TX. Inner commits even if outer fails -- use for audit logs. NESTED: savepoint within existing TX. Nested rollback only undoes nested changes; outer can catch and continue. Requires JDBC savepoint support.

q.ru: Why does calling @Transactional from within the same class not work? How to fix?
q.en: Why does calling @Transactional from within the same class not work? How to fix?

a.ru: Spring @Transactional works via AOP proxies. Self-invocation (A() calls B() in same class) is a direct Java call bypassing the proxy. Fixes: (1) Inject self and call self.B(); (2) Use TransactionTemplate; (3) Move B() to another @Service bean; (4) Use AspectJ compile-time weaving. This is the most common Spring transaction pitfall.
a.en: Spring @Transactional works via AOP proxies. Self-invocation (A() calls B() in same class) is a direct Java call bypassing the proxy. Fixes: (1) Inject self and call self.B(); (2) Use TransactionTemplate; (3) Move B() to another @Service bean; (4) Use AspectJ compile-time weaving. This is the most common Spring transaction pitfall.

summary.ru: Проблема N+1: один запрос для коллекции (N штук) + N запросов для ленивых связей. Решения: JOIN FETCH, @EntityGraph, @BatchSize. Пул соединений HikariCP управляет переиспользованием connection к БД.
summary.en: N+1 problem: one query for a collection (N items) plus N queries for lazy relations. Solutions: JOIN FETCH, @EntityGraph, @BatchSize. HikariCP connection pool manages DB connection reuse.

deepDive.ru:
## Проблема N+1

Возникает когда:
- 1 запрос для основной коллекции (N штук)
- N запросов для ленивой загрузки связей

**Решения:**
- **JOIN FETCH** -- подтянет дочерние записи одним запросом
- **@EntityGraph** -- декларативно указать какие связи подтянуть
- **@BatchSize** -- пакетная загрузка (IN clause)

## Пул соединений

Spring Boot по умолчанию использует **HikariCP**. Параметры: maximumPoolSize, minimumIdle, connectionTimeout, idleTimeout, maxLifeTime.
deepDive.en:
## Solutions in Detail

**JOIN FETCH:** `SELECT u FROM User u JOIN FETCH u.orders` -- one query with JOIN. Caution: cartesian product with multiple collections.

**@EntityGraph:** Declarative, per-query fetch control without modifying entity defaults.

**@BatchSize:** Global or per-field. Batch-loads collections for N parents in a single IN query.

**DTO Projections:** Avoid entities entirely -- no lazy loading, no N+1.

## HikariCP

Creating DB connections is expensive. HikariCP maintains reusable connections. Key: maximumPoolSize (default 10), connectionTimeout (30s), idleTimeout (10min), maxLifetime (30min).

tip.ru: Установите `default_batch_fetch_size=20` глобально как страховку, а для критичных запросов -- JOIN FETCH.
tip.en: Set `default_batch_fetch_size=20` globally as safety net, and use JOIN FETCH for critical queries.

q.ru: What is the N+1 problem in JPA/Hibernate?
q.en: What is the N+1 problem in JPA/Hibernate?

a.ru: N+1 occurs when loading N entities (1 query) and accessing a lazy association on each triggers N additional queries. Example: 100 users + getOrders() on each = 101 queries. Fix with JOIN FETCH, @EntityGraph, or @BatchSize.
a.en: N+1 occurs when loading N entities (1 query) and accessing a lazy association on each triggers N additional queries. Example: 100 users + getOrders() on each = 101 queries. Fix with JOIN FETCH, @EntityGraph, or @BatchSize.

q.ru: Compare JOIN FETCH, @EntityGraph, and @BatchSize for solving N+1.
q.en: Compare JOIN FETCH, @EntityGraph, and @BatchSize for solving N+1.

a.ru: JOIN FETCH: JPQL-based, one query via SQL JOIN. Issue: cartesian product with multiple collections. @EntityGraph: declarative, per-query override of lazy loading. @BatchSize: batch-loads collections in IN queries -- good as global safety net (default_batch_fetch_size=20). Best practice: @BatchSize globally + JOIN FETCH for critical paths.
a.en: JOIN FETCH: JPQL-based, one query via SQL JOIN. Issue: cartesian product with multiple collections. @EntityGraph: declarative, per-query override of lazy loading. @BatchSize: batch-loads collections in IN queries -- good as global safety net (default_batch_fetch_size=20). Best practice: @BatchSize globally + JOIN FETCH for critical paths.

q.ru: How would you detect and prevent N+1 problems in a large application?
q.en: How would you detect and prevent N+1 problems in a large application?

a.ru: Detection: (1) hibernate.generate_statistics=true for query counts. (2) datasource-proxy/p6spy in tests to assert query counts. (3) Hypersistence Optimizer for auto-detection. Prevention: (1) default_batch_fetch_size globally. (2) DTO projections for reads. (3) Code review: every findAll() accessing associations must use JOIN FETCH/EntityGraph. (4) @Fetch(FetchMode.SUBSELECT) for always-needed collections.
a.en: Detection: (1) hibernate.generate_statistics=true for query counts. (2) datasource-proxy/p6spy in tests to assert query counts. (3) Hypersistence Optimizer for auto-detection. Prevention: (1) default_batch_fetch_size globally. (2) DTO projections for reads. (3) Code review: every findAll() accessing associations must use JOIN FETCH/EntityGraph. (4) @Fetch(FetchMode.SUBSELECT) for always-needed collections.

summary.ru: Spring Security -- фреймворк аутентификации и авторизации. JWT (JSON Web Token) -- открытый стандарт для токенов доступа. Структура JWT: Header (тип, алгоритм), Payload (claims, данные пользователя), Signature (подпись). Header и Payload кодируются Base64, подпись создаётся указанным алгоритмом.
summary.en: Spring Security provides authentication and authorization. JWT (JSON Web Token) is a standard for access tokens. JWT structure: Header (type, algorithm), Payload (claims, user data), Signature. Header and Payload are Base64-encoded, signature is created with the specified algorithm.

deepDive.ru:
## Spring Security

Фреймворк для аутентификации и авторизации в Spring приложениях. Обрабатывает HTTP-запросы через цепочку фильтров (FilterChain).

## JWT

JSON Web Token -- открытый стандарт для создания токенов доступа на основе JSON. Используется для аутентификации в клиент-серверных приложениях.

**Структура:**
- **Header** -- тип токена, алгоритм подписи
- **Payload** -- полезные данные, информация по пользователю (JWT claims)
- **Signature** -- подпись токена

Header и Payload кодируются отдельно Base64. Затем алгоритмом из Header хэшируется header+payload с секретным ключом. Все три компонента объединяются через точку.
deepDive.en:
## Spring Security Architecture

Requests pass through a **SecurityFilterChain**. Key filters: `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter`, `BasicAuthenticationFilter`, custom JWT filter. The `AuthenticationManager` delegates to `AuthenticationProvider`s which use `UserDetailsService` to load users.

## JWT Deep Dive

**Why JWT?** Stateless authentication -- no server-side session storage. The token contains all needed info. Scales horizontally (any server can verify the token).

**JWT Structure:** `xxxxx.yyyyy.zzzzz`
- Header: `{"alg": "HS256", "typ": "JWT"}`
- Payload: `{"sub": "user123", "role": "ADMIN", "exp": 1700000000}`
- Signature: `HMACSHA256(base64(header) + "." + base64(payload), secret)`

**Standard claims:** `sub` (subject), `iss` (issuer), `exp` (expiration), `iat` (issued at), `aud` (audience).

**Access + Refresh token pattern:** Short-lived access token (15min) for API calls. Long-lived refresh token (7d) to obtain new access tokens without re-login.

**Security considerations:** Never store sensitive data in payload (it is only Base64-encoded, not encrypted). Always use HTTPS. Store tokens in httpOnly cookies (not localStorage) to prevent XSS.

tip.ru: Никогда не храните чувствительные данные в JWT payload -- он только закодирован Base64, а не зашифрован.
tip.en: Never store sensitive data in JWT payload -- it is only Base64-encoded, not encrypted.

q.ru: What is JWT and what are its three parts?
q.en: What is JWT and what are its three parts?

a.ru: JWT (JSON Web Token) is a standard for creating stateless access tokens. Three parts separated by dots: (1) Header -- token type and signing algorithm (e.g., HS256); (2) Payload -- claims (user data, roles, expiration); (3) Signature -- HMAC or RSA signature of encoded header + payload. Header and Payload are Base64-encoded (not encrypted), so never store secrets in them.
a.en: JWT (JSON Web Token) is a standard for creating stateless access tokens. Three parts separated by dots: (1) Header -- token type and signing algorithm (e.g., HS256); (2) Payload -- claims (user data, roles, expiration); (3) Signature -- HMAC or RSA signature of encoded header + payload. Header and Payload are Base64-encoded (not encrypted), so never store secrets in them.

q.ru: How does Spring Security's filter chain work with JWT authentication?
q.en: How does Spring Security's filter chain work with JWT authentication?

a.ru: Requests pass through a SecurityFilterChain. A custom JwtAuthFilter (extends OncePerRequestFilter) is added before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter. The filter: (1) extracts the Bearer token from the Authorization header; (2) validates the token (signature, expiration); (3) loads UserDetails; (4) creates an Authentication object and sets it in SecurityContextHolder. Subsequent filters/controllers can then access the authenticated user.
a.en: Requests pass through a SecurityFilterChain. A custom JwtAuthFilter (extends OncePerRequestFilter) is added before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter. The filter: (1) extracts the Bearer token from the Authorization header; (2) validates the token (signature, expiration); (3) loads UserDetails; (4) creates an Authentication object and sets it in SecurityContextHolder. Subsequent filters/controllers can then access the authenticated user.

q.ru: What are the security risks of JWT and how do you mitigate them?
q.en: What are the security risks of JWT and how do you mitigate them?

a.ru: Risks: (1) Token theft -- store in httpOnly cookies, not localStorage (XSS prevention). (2) No revocation -- JWTs are valid until expiry. Mitigate with short-lived access tokens (15min) + refresh tokens, or maintain a token blacklist in Redis. (3) Payload is not encrypted -- never include sensitive data. (4) Algorithm confusion attack -- always validate the algorithm server-side, never trust the header. (5) Secret key compromise -- use strong keys, rotate periodically. For high-security systems, consider opaque tokens with server-side session store instead of JWT.
a.en: Risks: (1) Token theft -- store in httpOnly cookies, not localStorage (XSS prevention). (2) No revocation -- JWTs are valid until expiry. Mitigate with short-lived access tokens (15min) + refresh tokens, or maintain a token blacklist in Redis. (3) Payload is not encrypted -- never include sensitive data. (4) Algorithm confusion attack -- always validate the algorithm server-side, never trust the header. (5) Secret key compromise -- use strong keys, rotate periodically. For high-security systems, consider opaque tokens with server-side session store instead of JWT.

summary.ru: AOP (Аспектно-ориентированное программирование) -- парадигма разделения сквозной функциональности (логирование, транзакции, безопасность) от бизнес-логики. Spring AOP использует прокси-объекты (JDK Dynamic Proxy для интерфейсов, CGLIB для классов).
summary.en: AOP (Aspect-Oriented Programming) separates cross-cutting concerns (logging, transactions, security) from business logic. Spring AOP uses proxy objects (JDK Dynamic Proxy for interfaces, CGLIB for classes).

deepDive.ru:
## Spring AOP

Аспектно-ориентированное программирование -- парадигма, основанная на разделении функциональности для улучшения модульности.

AOP позволяет внедрять повторяющуюся функциональность (логирование, транзакции) без изменения основного бизнес-кода.

Использует **прокси-объекты**:
- **CGLIB** -- наследование (проблема: final методы)
- **JDK Dynamic Proxy** -- через интерфейсы

Сквозная функциональность (cross-cutting concerns): логирование, обработка исключений, безопасность, транзакции.
deepDive.en:
## AOP Concepts

- **Aspect** -- a module encapsulating cross-cutting logic (e.g., logging aspect)
- **Join Point** -- a point in program execution (method call, exception throw)
- **Pointcut** -- an expression that selects join points (e.g., all methods in service layer)
- **Advice** -- the action taken at a join point. Types:
  - `@Before` -- runs before the method
  - `@After` -- runs after (regardless of outcome)
  - `@AfterReturning` -- runs after successful return
  - `@AfterThrowing` -- runs after exception
  - `@Around` -- wraps the method, controls execution
- **Weaving** -- linking aspects with target objects. Spring does this at runtime via proxies.

## Proxy Types

- **JDK Dynamic Proxy:** Used when the bean implements an interface. Proxy implements the same interface.
